Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to transform a raw Windows activity system log into a process mining event log This involves grouping events into cases Case ID defining standardized Activity Names and keeping Timestamps I also need to provide a brief explanation of the logic used

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Determine Case Identification Logic What are the logical units of work

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The prompt says A case can represent a logical unit of user work such as editing a specific document handling a particular email or reviewing a certain PDF This explicitly suggests artifact centric cases

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This is a direct instruction to use the specific document email or PDF as the Case ID I will follow this strictly

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Let us define cases by the primary window artifact that is the focus of the activity block

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait is it better to have ONE case for the whole Morning Work Session and use the Artifact as an attribute

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This explicitly endorses Artifact equals Case ID Interleaving is a known phenomenon in process mining We will keep Artifact as Case ID

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets refine Activity Names to be highly standardized and process mining friendly

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Open Artifact Edit Content Save Artifact Review Content Initiate Reply Send Message Analyze Content Update Data Resume Artifact Close Artifact

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets map events to these cases

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check for any missed events Total input lines 26 Total output rows 26 Matches perfectly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets ensure the explanation covers Case Identification Logic Grouped by primary artifact Activity Naming Logic Abstracted low level actions into standardized process mining activities Handling Interruptions noted with Resume Artifact
